case_name: Estate of David Spieker, by its Administrator, Diana Spieker, and Diana Spieker, individually v. Catholic Health Initiatives - Iowa Corp d/b/a Mercyone Des Moines Surgical Group and Dennis Witmer, D.O.

summary.brief: Estate of David Spieker, by its Administrator, Diana Spieker, and Diana Spieker, individually v. Catholic Health Initiatives - Iowa Corp d/b/a Mercyone Des Moines Surgical Group and Dennis Witmer, D.O.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The estate of David Spieker brought this action against the defendants in January 2021 after Spieker died of sepsis one day after a laparoscopic cholecystectomy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We find the estate failed to substantially comply with section 147.140.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The supreme court opted for a bright-line rule setting the dispositive-motion deadline as the deadline for raising challenges to the certificate of merit.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The court ordered dismissal of a medical malpractice action because it concluded the certificate of merit did not substantially comply with section 147.140.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The certificate did not include language that it was sworn 'under penalty of perjury,' and the estate’s expert was not under oath at the time of signing.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Affirming is insufficient. Miller, 7 N.W.3d at 375.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The estate did not comply with section 147.140 because their experts did not sign the certificate of merit under oath or under penalty of perjury.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The plain language of section 147.140(1) (2021) requires compliance 'prior to the commencement of discovery.'
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: We reject the estate’s challenge accordingly.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The estate argues substantial compliance because their expert 'affirmed' their statements.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
requestables.description: Surgical procedure performed on the patient

reveal.result_summary: Procedure performed, patient died of sepsis one day after
reveal.decision_impact: Critical in understanding cause of death

decision_points.prompt: What post-operative monitoring should be conducted?

options.description: Regular monitoring for signs of infection
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard post-operative care to detect complications early
Insufficient to detect early signs of sepsis

expected_action_court.description: Regular monitoring for signs of infection
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established need for close monitoring

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to monitor led to undetected sepsis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Regular monitoring could have identified sepsis early

patient_outcome.description: Patient died of sepsis one day after surgery

key_findings: Certificate of merit did not comply with statutory requirements

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Procedural non-compliance with certificate of merit

court_findings.finding: Certificate of merit did not comply with statutory requirements
